text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Nɔvinyewo, migawɔ nu baɖa o!
translated: And you said: Young people, I have done nothing wrong!

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Ke esi Laban yi fu sẽ ge na lãwo la, Raxel fi fofoa ƒe aklama kpakpɛwo.
translated: When Laban went to shear his sheep, Rachel stole her father's household idols.

ref: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
text: Ame siawo nye Ismaelviwo kple woƒe ŋkɔwo. Fia wuieve le woƒe aƒetsotsowo, agbadɔwo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.
translated: These people are Ishmaelites and their descendants. Two kings ruled over their families, their armies and their country.

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Ke Abram gblɔ na Sodom-fia bena. Meta Yehowa, Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ, si wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba la
translated: When Abram spoke to the king of Sodom, he said: “I have lifted my hand to the Lord, God Most High, Possessor of heaven and earth.”

ref: the third was Basemath, who was Ishmael's daughter and Nebaioth's sister.
text: kple Basmat, Ismael ƒe vinyɔnu, si enye Nebayot ƒe nɔvinyɔnu.
translated: with Basmat, Ishmael's daughter-in-law, who is Nebaioth's daughter-in-law.

ref: Two years after the flood, when Shem was
text: Esi Sem dzi Arpaxsad megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa atɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.
translated: After Shem was born Arpachshad, he lived four hundred and three years, and he begat sons and daughters.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Tete Abram gblɔ na Lot bena: Megana dzre nanɔ mía kpli wò, nye lãnyilawo kple wò lãnyilawo dome o, elabena nɔviwo míenye.
translated: Then Abram said to Lot: Let there be no strife between me and you, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen, for we are brothers.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Eye Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fiae le Mamre ƒe ati gãwo gbɔ, le esime wòbɔbɔ nɔ eƒe agbadɔ nu le ŋdɔ me.
translated: And Jehovah appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat at the tent door in the heat of the day.

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Esia nede megbe xaa tso gbɔwò be, nagawu ame dzɔdzɔe kple ame vɔ̃ɖi siaa zi ɖeka, eye ame dzɔdzɔe nanɔ abe ame vɔ̃ɖi ene o! Esia nede megbe xaa tso gbɔwò! Ɖe xexeame ƒe ʋɔnudrɔ̃la madrɔ̃ ʋɔnu dzɔdzɔe oa?
translated: This is what the world has come to: a poor person and a rich person are treated the same, yet the poor person is not like the rich one! This is what the world has come to! In these times, does the judge judge fairly?

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Le ŋkeke ma dzi Yehowa bla nu kple Abram gblɔ bena: Wò dzidzimeviwo matsɔ anyigba sia, si tso Egipte-tɔsisi la nu vaseɖe Frat-tɔsisi gã la nu ana:
translated: On that day Jehovah said to Abram: Lift up your eyes and look from the place where you are, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates:

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Tsɔe vɛ nam, ne maɖu vinye ƒe adelã la, eye mayra wò! Tete wòtsɔe ve nɛ woɖu, emegbe wotsɔ wein vɛ nɛ wònɔ.
translated: And you said: "Take some and let me taste your sweetness, and I will love you!" First you took and ate, then you took wine and drank.

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Ke esi Israel le nuto sia me la, Ruben de asi fofoa ƒe kosi Bilha ŋu, eye Israel see.
translated: When Israel was living in that land, Reuben went and slept with his father’s concubine Bilhah, and Israel heard about it.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Ale miagblɔ na nye aƒetɔ Esau enye si: Wò dɔla Yakob be, míagblɔ na wò bena: Menɔ Laban gbɔ, eye metsi anyi ʋuu vaseɖe fifi.
translated: And you said to your servant: "Bring me to your brother Esau." But Jacob deceived you, saying: "I will go to Laban," and he sat down and wept bitterly.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Mia ŋutɔwo mienyae bena, nye ŋusẽ katã metsɔ subɔ mia fofo lae.
translated: When your elders are talking, I, the youngest child, do not interrupt your father's speech.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Yakob bena: Trɔ yi fofowò ƒe anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ, eye manɔ anyi kpli wò!
translated: And Jehovah said to Jacob: Return to the land of your fathers and to your relatives, and I will be with you!
